options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

**Items:** ensure all logically relevant items can be acquired. Some items, such as keys, may be self-locking, and
some locations may be inaccessible.

options.name: plando_connections
options.description:
Generic connections plando. Format is:
- entrance: "Entrance Name"
  exit: "Exit Name"
  direction: "Direction"
  percentage: 100
Direction must be one of 'entrance', 'exit', or 'both', and defaults to 'both' if omitted.
Percentage is an integer from 1 to 100, and defaults to 100 when omitted.

options.name: plando_texts
options.description:
Text plando. Format is:
- text: 'This is your text'
  at: text_key
  percentage: 100
Percentage is an integer from 1 to 100, and defaults to 100 when omitted.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Ganon: Climb GT, defeat Agahnim 2, and then kill Ganon
Crystals: Only killing Ganon is required. However, items may still be placed in GT
Bosses: Defeat the boss of all dungeons, including Agahnim's tower and GT (Aga 2)
Pedestal: Pull the Triforce from the Master Sword pedestal
Ganon Pedestal: Pull the Master Sword pedestal, then kill Ganon
Triforce Hunt: Collect Triforce pieces spread throughout the worlds, then turn them in to Murahadala in front of Hyrule Castle
Local Triforce Hunt: Collect Triforce pieces spread throughout your world, then turn them in to Murahadala in front of Hyrule Castle
Ganon Triforce Hunt: Collect Triforce pieces spread throughout the worlds, then kill Ganon
Local Ganon Triforce Hunt: Collect Triforce pieces spread throughout your world, then kill Ganon

options.name: mode
options.description:
Standard: Begin the game by rescuing Zelda from her cell and escorting her to the Sanctuary
Open: Begin the game from your choice of Link's House or the Sanctuary
Inverted: Begin in the Dark World. The Moon Pearl is required to avoid bunny-state in Light World, and the Light World game map is altered

options.name: glitches_required
options.description:
Determine the logic required to complete the seed
None: No glitches required
Minor Glitches: Puts fake flipper, waterwalk, super bunny shenanigans, and etc into logic
Overworld Glitches: Assumes the player has knowledge of both overworld major glitches (boots clips, mirror clips) and minor glitches
Hybrid Major Glitches: In addition to overworld glitches, also requires underworld clips between dungeons.
No Logic: Your own items are placed with no regard to any logic; such as your Fire Rod can be on your Trinexx.

options.name: dark_room_logic
options.description:
Logic for unlit dark rooms. Lamp: require the Lamp for these rooms to be considered accessible.
Torches: in addition to lamp, allow the fire rod and presence of easily accessible torches for access.
None: all dark rooms are always considered doable, meaning this may force completion of rooms in complete darkness.

options.name: open_pyramid
options.description:
Determines whether the hole at the top of pyramid is open.
Goal will open the pyramid if the goal requires you to kill Ganon, without needing to kill Agahnim 2.
Auto is the same as goal except if Ganon's dropdown is in another location, the hole will be closed.

options.name: crystals_needed_for_gt
options.description: Number of crystals needed to open Ganon's Tower

options.name: crystals_needed_for_ganon
options.description: Number of crystals needed to damage Ganon

options.name: triforce_pieces_mode
options.description:
Determine how to calculate the extra available triforce pieces.
Extra: available = triforce_pieces_extra + triforce_pieces_required
Percentage: available = (triforce_pieces_percentage /100) * triforce_pieces_required
Available: available = triforce_pieces_available

options.name: triforce_pieces_percentage
options.description: Set to how many triforce pieces according to a percentage of the required ones, are available to collect in the world.

options.name: triforce_pieces_required
options.description:
Set to how many out of X triforce pieces you need to win the game in a triforce hunt.
Default is 20. Max is 90, Min is 1.

options.name: triforce_pieces_available
options.description: Set to how many triforces pieces are available to collect in the world. Default is 30. Max is 90, Min is 1

options.name: triforce_pieces_extra
options.description: Set to how many extra triforces pieces are available to collect in the world.

options.name: entrance_shuffle
options.description:
Dungeons Simple: Shuffle just dungeons amongst each other, swapping dungeons entirely, so Hyrule Castle is always 1 dungeon.
Dungeons Full: Shuffle any dungeon entrance with any dungeon interior, so Hyrule Castle can be 4 different dungeons, but keep dungeons to a specific world.
Dungeons Crossed: like dungeons_full, but allow cross-world traversal through a dungeon. Warning: May force repeated dungeon traversal.
Simple: Entrances are grouped together before being randomized. Interiors with two entrances are grouped shuffled together with each other,
and Death Mountain entrances are shuffled only on Death Mountain. Dungeons are swapped entirely.
Restricted: Like Simple, but single entrance interiors, multi entrance interiors, and Death Mountain interior entrances are all shuffled with each other.
Full: Like Restricted, but all Dungeon entrances are shuffled with all non-Dungeon entrances.
Crossed: Like Full, but interiors with multiple entrances are no longer confined to the same world, which may allow crossing worlds.
Insanity: Like Crossed, but entrances and exits may be decoupled from each other, so that leaving through an exit may not return you to the entrance you entered from.

options.name: entrance_shuffle_seed
options.description:
You can specify a number to use as an entrance shuffle seed, or a group name. Everyone with the same group name
will get the same entrance shuffle result as long as their Entrance Shuffle, Mode, Retro Caves, and Glitches
Required options are the same.

options.name: big_key_shuffle
options.description: Big Key Placement

options.name: small_key_shuffle
options.description: Small Key Placement

options.name: key_drop_shuffle
options.description:
Shuffle keys found in pots and dropped from killed enemies,
respects the small key and big key shuffle options.

options.name: compass_shuffle
options.description: Compass Placement

options.name: map_shuffle
options.description: Map Placement

options.name: restrict_dungeon_item_on_boss
options.description: Don't place dungeon-native items on the dungeon's boss.

options.name: item_pool
options.description:
Easy: Doubled upgrades, progressives, and etc. Normal:  Item availability remains unchanged from vanilla game.
Hard: Reduced upgrade availability (max: 14 hearts, blue mail, tempered sword, fire shield, no silvers unless swordless).
Expert: Minimum upgrade availability (max: 8 hearts, green mail, master sword, fighter shield, no silvers unless swordless).

options.name: item_functionality
options.description:
Easy: Allow Hammer to damage ganon, Allow Hammer tablet collection, Allow swordless medallion use everywhere.
Normal: Vanilla item functionality
Hard: Reduced helpfulness of items (potions less effective, can't catch faeries, cape uses double magic, byrna does not grant invulnerability, boomerangs do not stun, silvers disabled outside ganon)
Expert: Vastly reduces the helpfulness of items (potions barely effective, can't catch faeries, cape uses double magic, byrna does not grant invulnerability, boomerangs and hookshot do not stun, silvers disabled outside ganon)

options.name: enemy_health
options.description:
Default: Vanilla enemy HP. Easy: Enemies have reduced health. Hard: Enemies have increased health.
Expert: Enemies have greatly increased health.

options.name: enemy_damage
options.description:
Default: Vanilla enemy damage. Shuffled: 0 # Enemies deal 0 to 4 hearts and armor helps.
Chaos: Enemies deal 0 to 8 hearts and armor just reshuffles the damage.

options.name: progressive
options.description: How item types that have multiple tiers (armor, bows, gloves, shields, and swords) should be rewarded

options.name: swordless
options.description:
No swords. Curtains in Skull Woods and Agahnim's
Tower are removed, Agahnim's Tower barrier can be
destroyed with hammer. Misery Mire and Turtle Rock
can be opened without a sword. Hammer damages Ganon.
Ether and Bombos Tablet can be activated with Hammer
(and Book).

options.name: dungeon_counters
options.description:
On: Always display amount of items checked in a dungeon. Pickup: Show when compass is picked up.
Default: Show when compass is picked up if the compass itself is shuffled. Off: Never show item count in dungeons.

options.name: retro_bow
options.description: Zelda-1 like mode. You have to purchase a quiver to shoot arrows using rupees.

options.name: retro_caves
options.description:
Zelda-1 like mode. There are randomly placed take-any caves that contain one Sword and
choices of Heart Container/Blue Potion.

options.name: hints
options.description: On/Full: Put item and entrance placement hints on telepathic tiles and some NPCs, Full removes joke hints.

options.name: scams
options.description: If on, these Merchants will no longer tell you what they're selling.

options.name: boss_shuffle
options.description:
Shuffles bosses around to different locations.
Basic will shuffle all bosses except Ganon and Agahnim anywhere they can be placed.
Full chooses 3 bosses at random to be placed twice instead of Lanmolas, Moldorm, and Helmasaur.
Chaos allows any boss to appear any number of times.
Singularity places a single boss in as many places as possible, and a second boss in any remaining locations.
Supports plando placement.

options.name: pot_shuffle
options.description: Shuffle contents of pots within "supertiles" (item will still be nearby original placement).

options.name: enemy_shuffle
options.description:
Randomize every enemy spawn.
If mode is Standard, Hyrule Castle is left out (may result in visually wrong enemy sprites in that area.)

options.name: killable_thieves
options.description: Makes Thieves killable.

options.name: bush_shuffle
options.description: Randomize chance that a bush contains an enemy as well as which enemy may spawn.

options.name: shop_item_slots
options.description: Number of slots in all shops available to have items from the multiworld

options.name: randomize_shop_inventories
options.description: Generate new default inventories for overworld/underworld shops, and unique shops; or each shop independently

options.name: shuffle_shop_inventories
options.description: Shuffle default inventories of the shops around

options.name: include_witch_hut
options.description: Consider witch's hut like any other shop and shuffle/randomize it too

options.name: randomize_shop_prices
options.description: Randomize the prices of the items in shop inventories

options.name: randomize_cost_types
options.description: Prices of the items in shop inventories may cost hearts, arrow, or bombs instead of rupees

options.name: shop_price_modifier
options.description: Percentage modifier for shuffled item prices in shops

options.name: shuffle_capacity_upgrades
options.description:
Shuffle capacity upgrades into the item pool (and allow them to traverse the multiworld).
On Combined will shuffle only a single bomb upgrade and arrow upgrade each which bring you to the maximum capacity.

options.name: bombless_start
options.description: Start with a max of 0 bombs available, requiring Bomb Upgrade items in order to use bombs

options.name: shuffle_prizes
options.description: Shuffle "general" prize packs, as in enemy, tree pull, dig etc.; "bonk" prizes; or both.

options.name: tile_shuffle
options.description: Randomize flying tiles floor patterns.

options.name: misery_mire_medallion
options.description: Required medallion to open Misery Mire front entrance.

options.name: turtle_rock_medallion
options.description: Required medallion to open Turtle Rock front entrance.

options.name: glitch_boots
options.description: If this is enabled, the player will start with Pegasus Boots when playing with overworld glitches or harder logic.

options.name: beemizer_total_chance
options.description:
Percentage chance for each junk-fill item (rupees, bombs, arrows) to be
replaced with either a bee swarm trap or a single bottle-filling bee.

options.name: beemizer_trap_chance
options.description:
Percentage chance for each replaced junk-fill item to be a bee swarm
trap; all other replaced items are single bottle-filling bees.

options.name: timer
options.description:
None: No timer will be displayed. OHKO: Timer always at zero. Permanent OHKO.
Timed: Starts with clock at zero. Green clocks subtract 4 minutes (total 20). Blue clocks subtract 2 minutes (total 10). Red clocks add two minutes (total 10). Winner is the player with the lowest time at the end.
Timed OHKO: Starts the clock at ten minutes. Green clocks add five minutes (total 25). As long as the clock as at zero, Link will die in one hit.
Timed Countdown: Starts the clock with forty minutes. Same clocks as timed mode, but if the clock hits zero you lose. You can still keep playing, though.
Display: Displays a timer, but otherwise does not affect gameplay or the item pool.

options.name: countdown_start_time
options.description: For Timed OHKO and Timed Countdown timer modes, the amount of time in minutes to start with.

options.name: red_clock_time
options.description: For all timer modes, the amount of time in minutes to gain or lose when picking up a red clock.

options.name: blue_clock_time
options.description: For all timer modes, the amount of time in minutes to gain or lose when picking up a blue clock.

options.name: green_clock_time
options.description: For all timer modes, the amount of time in minutes to gain or lose when picking up a green clock.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: allow_collect
options.display_name: Allow Collection of checks for other players
options.description: Allows for !collect / co-op to auto-open chests containing items for other players.

options.name: ow_palettes
options.description: The type of palette shuffle to use for the overworld

options.name: uw_palettes
options.description: The type of palette shuffle to use for the underworld (caves, dungeons, etc.)

options.name: hud_palettes
options.description: The type of palette shuffle to use for the HUD

options.name: sword_palettes
options.description: The type of palette shuffle to use for the sword

options.name: shield_palettes
options.description: The type of palette shuffle to use for the shield

options.name: heartbeep
options.description: How quickly the heart beep sound effect will play

options.name: heartcolor
options.description: The color of hearts in the HUD

options.name: quickswap
options.description: Allows you to quickly swap items while playing with L/R

options.name: menuspeed
options.description: How quickly the menu appears/disappears

options.name: music
options.description: Whether background music will play in game

options.name: reduceflashing
options.description: Reduces flashing for certain scenes such as the Misery Mire and Ganon's Tower opening cutscenes

options.name: triforcehud
options.description: When and how the triforce hunt HUD should display

options.name: goals
options.description: This Option has been Removed.

options.name: smallkey_shuffle
options.description: This Option has been Removed.

options.name: bigkey_shuffle
options.description: This Option has been Removed.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
